2604.27618.title: Math Education Digital Shadows for facilitating learning with LLMs: Math performance, anxiety and confidence in simulated students and AIs
2604.27618.paper_summary: To enhance LLMs' impact on math education, we need data on their mathematical prowess and biases across prompts. To fill this gap, we introduce MEDS (Math Education Digital Shadows) as a dataset mapping how large language models reason about and report mathematics across human- and AI-like conditions. MEDS involves 28,000 personas from 14 LLMs (from families like Mistral, Qwen, DeepSeek, Granite, Phi and Grok) shadowing either humans or AI assistants. Each record/shadow includes a set of prompts along with psychological/sociodemographic persona metadata and four types of math tasks: (i) open math interview, (ii) three psychometric tests about math perceptions with explanations, (iii) cognitive networks capturing math attitudes, and (iv) 18 high-school math test questions together with their reasoning and confidence scores. MEDS differs from traditional score-only math benchmarks because it integrates concepts of self-efficacy, math anxiety, and cognitive network science besides math proficiency scores. Data validation shows that the sampled LLMs exhibit schema integrity and consistent personas, together with family-specific peculiarities like human-like negative math attitudes, logical fallacies, and math overconfidence. MEDS will benefit learning analytics experts, cognitive scientists, and developers of safer AI tutors in mathematics.
2604.27618.paper_summary_zh: 為了增強大型語言模型（LLMs）對數學教育的影響，我們需要有關它們在各種提示下的數學能力和偏見的數據。為填補這一空白，我們引入了MEDS（數學教育數位影子）作為一個數據集，映射大型語言模型如何在類人和類AI的條件下推理和報告數學。MEDS 涉及來自14個LLM（如Mistral、Qwen、DeepSeek、Granite、Phi和Grok等家族）的28,000個角色，這些角色影射人類或AI助手。每條記錄/影子包括一組提示，並附有心理/社會人口統計角色元數據以及四種類型的數學任務：（i）開放式數學面試，（ii）三個關於數學感知的心理測量測試及其解釋，（iii）捕捉數學態度的認知網絡，以及（iv）18道高中數學測試問題及其推理和信心分數。MEDS 與傳統的僅有分數的數學基準不同，因為它整合了自我效能、數學焦慮和認知網絡科學的概念，除了數學能力分數之外。數據驗證顯示，抽樣的LLMs展現了架構完整性和一致的角色，並具有特定家族的特徵，如類人負面數學態度、邏輯謬誤和數學過度自信。MEDS 將使學習分析專家、認知科學家和數學安全AI導師的開發者受益。
2604.27618.author: Naomi Esposito et.al.
2604.27618.authors: Naomi Esposito, Anthony Tricarico, Luisa Porzio, Ali Aghazadeh Ardebili, Massimo Stella
